[2018-07-27 04:46] shadourow (Rando): Nope, because most of the time, shiver don't do actual damage
[2018-07-27 04:46] shadourow (Rando): When it does, you're indeed right
[2018-07-27 04:47] junkmail: Yes, but shiver does the best part of rhino better
[2018-07-27 04:47] junkmail: Ie the threat/gambit pressure
[2018-07-27 04:47] junkmail: It's even sorta vigilant
[2018-07-27 04:48] shadourow (Rando): I don't agree, you can basically counter 2 threat by holding 2 drones (worse case), and that cost 2 golds, while 1 damage is also worth around 2 golds
[2018-07-27 04:48] shadourow (Rando): And ofc, most of the times, you have better options to deal with 2 threat
[2018-07-27 04:48] junkmail: If you kill the drones its worth more than 2 gold
[2018-07-27 04:49] junkmail: If you click rhino to make them buy an extra husk vs freezing a forcefield to make them buy 2 more husks
[2018-07-27 04:49] junkmail: So rhino does 2 damage eventually, yeti does 2 immediayelt
[2018-07-27 04:49] junkmail: Of course this falls apart with breachproof and shit
[2018-07-27 04:50] shadourow (Rando): In your situation, indeed, both units deal 2 damage, but yeti in one turn while rhino to it in two, but then, you can sac the rhino
[2018-07-27 04:50] junkmail: But 2 freeze permanently is worth a little more than 2 damage
[2018-07-27 04:50] junkmail: Since freezing for finesse and all
[2018-07-27 04:51] shadourow (Rando): And... judging why rhino is better than shiver in your situation involve inflation calculus 😐
[2018-07-27 04:51] junkmail: 2 damage now > 1 now 1 later is the simplest imaginable inflation calculus
[2018-07-27 04:52] shadourow (Rando): So I guess that :
1 damage now, 1 damage a turn later, 2 soak two turns later > 2 damage now
is also obviously obvious
[2018-07-27 04:52] junkmail: 2 damage now, eventually 2 soak later
[2018-07-27 04:53] junkmail: Paying back the 2 damage deficif
[2018-07-27 04:53] shadourow (Rando): But you'll lose the damage to gain the soak
[2018-07-27 04:53] junkmail: But also you can "do" 2 damage while leaving yeti unclicked etc etc
[2018-07-27 04:53] junkmail: w/e prismata is spooky and complicated
[2018-07-27 04:53] junkmail: Point is that yeti is fast which is often better
[2018-07-27 04:54] extratricky: a good rule of thumb is that if the rhino/shiver is dying on defense then shiver is better, and if it's not then rhino is better
[2018-07-27 04:54] shadourow (Rando): That point is right, but other point is that soak is good
[2018-07-27 04:55] shadourow (Rando): But tbh, both dying immediacy on defense are pretty bad
[2018-07-27 04:55] junkmail: Eh shiver dying is fine
[2018-07-27 04:55] junkmail: Since it creates gambit pressure if you choose to block on ot
[2018-07-27 04:55] shadourow (Rando): I guess it's almost ok, indeed
[2018-07-27 04:55] extratricky: well they're both usually better than holding drones, which means that it happens a lot
[2018-07-27 04:55] junkmail: Probably a better soak unit than husk~
[2018-07-27 04:57] shadourow (Rando): Husk indeed isn't good neither ^^' But I like it to avoid to be exploited, but obviously, even if you need to sink red, that's not really good
[2018-07-27 04:57] junkmail: Husk sux but it also should not be a good unit
[2018-07-27 04:57] shadourow (Rando): Yup, red should be weak in defense
[2018-07-27 04:58] junkmail: Nah its not so much red should be weak on defense as prompt 1 health defenders with no downside should suck
[2018-07-27 04:58] junkmail: So as to make offensive finesse/tactics more possible
[2018-07-27 04:58] namington: husk is bad, but if husk were good, itd be broken when red is free

[2018-07-27 04:59] namington: thats part of the problem with low-cost units tho, at that level its not "specific" enough to do, like, 1-gold cost adjustments
[2018-07-27 04:59] namington: without totally throwing balance out of whack
[2018-07-27 04:59] junkmail: Corpus is good soak but thats sorta fine
[2018-07-27 04:59] junkmail: Since its more finnicky and leads to more interesting situations
[2018-07-27 04:59] namington: if we could take like, 0.2 gold off of husk or smthn
[2018-07-27 05:00] shadourow (Rando): Imagine Prismata, but not bounded by natural numbers :monkaS:
[2018-07-27 05:00] namington: prismata in the complex plane when
[2018-07-27 05:01] junkmail: i summon 7e^(iπ/4) husks
[2018-07-27 05:01] namington: "damn, centurion costs 4.76155 - 7.6605i gold, but I only have 5.15312 - 6.8821i gold saved up"
[2018-07-27 05:02] namington: if prismata were played in the complex plane
[2018-07-27 05:02] shadourow (Rando): Oops, I just realised that |C is actually just R²
[2018-07-27 05:02] namington: itd probably be more convenient to describe it in polar cordinates
[2018-07-27 05:02] shadourow (Rando): And that prismata is actually |R^5
[2018-07-27 05:03] shadourow (Rando): :Blue: :Red: :Green: :Gold: :Energy:
[2018-07-27 05:03] namington: the first statement, that is
[2018-07-27 05:03] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): prismata is actually 10-dimensional
[2018-07-27 05:03] namington: the effects of complex powers (like n^i) cant be simulated in R^2
[2018-07-27 05:03] namington: as well as many logarithmic results
[2018-07-27 05:04] shadourow (Rando): You don't have multiplication in Prismata, having |C instead of |R² is pointless
[2018-07-27 05:04] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): the normal 4 dimension being RGBE and the remaining 6 is the calabi-yau surface that represents gold
[2018-07-27 05:04] namington: @Rando what we need is a new unit that reads "Click: Gain sqrt(n) gold, where n is the amount of Engineers you lost during your opponent's last turn"
[2018-07-27 05:05] namington: if your opponent bought arms race, then n is logically negative
[2018-07-27 05:05] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): just make people calculate integrals for unit's damage
[2018-07-27 05:05] shadourow (Rando): Also, consider that you can imagine to lose engies
[2018-07-27 05:05] shadourow (Rando): Prismata is a game of morale
[2018-07-27 05:05] shadourow (Rando): The player with the best imagination wins
[2018-07-27 05:07] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): my girlfriend is like $10E^{\frac{i\pi}{2}}$
[2018-07-27 05:07] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): :GWvertiPeepoSadMan:
[2018-07-27 05:07] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): prismata seems to be a nerd magnet tbh
